task_updates.executor_notes: Read-only audit completed. Grep `post_simple_topic|post_sectioned_topic` across `src/` and `tests/` found the expected topic-editor surface plus runtime/core test coverage. Code touch points verified in `src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py`: `WRITE_TOOL_NAMES` currently includes `post_simple_topic`, `post_sectioned_topic`, `watch_topic`, `update_topic_source_messages`, `discard_topic`, `record_observation`, `finalize_run` and does not include `post_topic`; `TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` still uses the role label `BNDC live-update topic editor`, advertises decision tools as `post_simple_topic, post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic, update_topic_source_messages, discard_topic, record_observation`, and contains the media-to-sectioned steering under `## Structured Document Topics (post_sectioned_topic with blocks)` saying multi-section topics and ANY image/video/embed/external media link should use `post_sectioned_topic` blocks; `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS` defines `post_simple_topic` as text-only with media forbidden and `post_sectioned_topic` as the structured/media-capable tool with required `body` and `source_message_ids`; dispatch routes only `{post_simple_topic, post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic}` to `_dispatch_create_topic_tool`; normalized block writeback currently only writes `args["blocks"]` for `post_sectioned_topic`; `action_by_tool` lacks `post_topic`; legacy `post_simple_topic` media/raw-media-ref guard still rejects with `post_simple_cannot_attach_media_use_post_sectioned_topic`; legacy `post_simple_topic` source/author-count guard still rejects multi-source/multi-author simple posts; current `post_sectioned_topic` blocks-required guard accepts either non-empty legacy `sections` or normalized `blocks`; collision rejected-action map covers only simple/sectioned/watch; `_tool_summary` publish summary recognizes only simple/sectioned/watch; `_summary_for_tool` stores legacy `post_sectioned_topic` as `{body, sections, blocks?}` and simple/default as `{body, media}`; auto-shortlist `summary.suggested_actions` still tells the model `post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic`; `build_rejected_transition` allows only `rejected_post_simple`, `rejected_post_sectioned`, and `rejected_watch`; idempotency/retry helpers also have write-tool sets that will need `post_topic` in later tasks. Publisher path verified: `_publish_topic` treats any topic whose summary has `blocks` as structured, hydrates source metadata, calls `render_topic_publish_units`, then builds send units. `render_topic_publish_units` normalizes via `normalize_topic_document`; for a one-block intro topic it emits exactly one text unit containing `## Live update: <headline>` plus the intro text and per-block sources, then emits one media/external unit per resolvable `media_ref`. The intro branch intentionally skips `**title**` section markup because title lines are only added when `block["type"] == "section" and block_title`; no `Share with your followers` string exists in this repo by grep, so no duplicate CTA path was found here. Tests enumerated: `tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py` contains direct runtime assertions, including the media-bearing simple rejection at lines around 305-337, tool-list equality/length around 301, action vocabulary audit around 1380-1437, legacy simple calls, and several `post_sectioned_topic` blocks/media validation/publishing tests around 2140 onward; `tests/test_topic_editor_core.py` contains `render_topic_publish_units`, `normalize_document_blocks`, media-ref normalization, and rejected-transition core tests; `tests/test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py` checks topic-editor tool schema registration for vision tools; `tests/test_backfill_live_update_topics.py`, `tests/test_live_update_editor_publishing.py`, and `tests/test_live_update_editor_lifecycle.py` have no direct grep hits for `post_simple_topic`, `post_sectioned_topic`, `render_topic_publish_units`, `normalize_document_blocks`, or `build_rejected_transition`; `tests/test_live_update_editor_*` remain relevant as related editor suites for later full verification. Grep found no tests asserting obsolete prompt strings like `use sectioned for ANY media` or `BNDC live-update topic editor`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: SC1 satisfied: audit explicitly enumerated WRITE_TOOL_NAMES, TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT, TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS, writeback, legacy media/source-count guards, blocks-required guard, rejected-action map, publish path, _summary_for_tool, auto-shortlist suggested_actions, build_rejected_transition, render_topic_publish_units, and the requested test files/ranges.
